heading: Energy efficiency for data center buildings
label: 17112 Energy efficiency for data center buildings

children.label: A
children.text: a free-standing structure; or

children.label: B
children.text: a facility within a larger structure, that uses environmental control equipment to maintain the proper conditions for the operation of electronic equipment.

children.heading: Data center operator
children.label: 2 Data center operator
children.text: The term “data center operator” means any person or government entity that builds or operates a data center or purchases data center services, equipment, and facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency shall, after consulting with information technology industry and other interested parties, initiate a voluntary national information program for those types of data centers and data center equipment and facilities that are widely used and for which there is a potential for significant data center energy savings as a result of the program.December 19, 2007

children.label: A
children.text: address data center efficiency holistically, reflecting the total energy consumption of data centers as whole systems, including both equipment and facilities;

children.label: B
children.text: consider prior work and studies undertaken in this area, including by the Environmental Protection Agency and the Department of Energy;

children.label: C
children.text: consistent with the objectives described in paragraph (1), determine the type of data center and data center equipment and facilities to be covered under the program;

children.label: i
children.text: the performance and use of servers, data storage devices, and other information technology equipment;

children.label: ii
children.text: the efficiency of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning, cooling, and power conditioning systems, provided that no modification shall be required of a standard then in effect under the Energy Policy and Conservation Act ( et seq.) for any covered heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, cooling or power-conditioning product;42 U.S.C. 6201

children.label: iii
children.text: energy savings from the adoption of software and data management techniques; and

children.label: iv
children.text: other factors proposed by the stakeholders described in subsection (c);

children.label: E
children.text: allow for creation of separate specifications, measurements, and benchmarks based on data center size and function, as well as other appropriate characteristics;

children.label: F
children.text: advance the design and implementation of efficiency technologies to the maximum extent economically practical;

children.label: G
children.text: provide to data center operators in the private sector and the Federal Government information about best practices and purchasing decisions that reduce the energy consumption of data centers; and

children.label: H
children.text: publish the information described in subparagraph (G), which may be disseminated through catalogs, trade publications, the Internet, or other mechanisms, that will allow data center operators to assess the energy consumption and potential cost savings of alternative data centers and data center equipment and facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary and the Administrator shall carry out subsection (b) in collaboration with the information technology industry and other key stakeholders, with the goal of producing results that accurately reflect the most relevant and useful information.

children.label: A
children.text: have members with expertise in energy efficiency and in the development, operation, and functionality of data centers, information technology equipment, and software, including representatives of hardware manufacturers, data center operators, and facility managers;

children.label: B
children.text: obtain and address input from the National Laboratories (as that term is defined in section 15801) of this title, or any institution of higher education, research institution, industry association, company, or public interest group with applicable expertise;

children.label: i
children.text: commonly accepted procedures for the development of specifications; and

children.label: ii
children.text: accredited standards development processes; or

children.label: D
children.text: have a mission to promote energy efficiency for data centers and information technology.

children.heading: Measurements and specifications
children.label: d Measurements and specifications
children.text: The Secretary and the Administrator shall consider and assess the adequacy of the specifications, measurements, best practices, and benchmarks described in subsection (b) for use by the Federal Energy Management Program, the Energy Star Program, and other efficiency programs of the Department of Energy or the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.heading: Definition of report
children.label: 1 Definition of report
children.text: In this subsection, the term “report” means the report of the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory entitled “United States Data Center Energy Usage Report” and dated June 2016, which was prepared as an update to the “Report to Congress on Server and Data Center Energy Efficiency”, published on , pursuant to ().August 2, 2007section 1 of Public Law 109–431120 Stat. 2920

children.label: A
children.text: a comparison and gap analysis of the estimates and projections contained in the report with new data regarding the period from 2015 through 2019;

children.label: B
children.text: an analysis considering the impact of information technologies, including virtualization and cloud computing, in the public and private sectors;

children.label: C
children.text: an evaluation of the impact of the combination of cloud platforms, mobile devices, social media, and big data on data center energy usage;

children.label: D
children.text: an evaluation of water usage in data centers and recommendations for reductions in that water usage; and

children.label: E
children.text: updated projections and recommendations for best practices through fiscal year 2025.

children.heading: Data center energy practitioner program
children.label: f Data center energy practitioner program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, in collaboration with key stakeholders and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, shall maintain a data center energy practitioner program that provides for the certification of energy practitioners qualified to evaluate the energy usage and efficiency opportunities in federally owned and operated data centers.

children.heading: Evaluations
children.label: 2 Evaluations
children.text: Each Federal agency shall consider having the data centers of the agency evaluated once every 4 years by energy practitioners certified pursuant to the program, whenever practicable using certified practitioners employed by the agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, in collaboration with key stakeholders and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, shall establish an open data initiative relating to energy usage at federally owned and operated data centers, with the purpose of making the data available and accessible in a manner that encourages further data center innovation, optimization, and consolidation.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: 2 Consideration
children.text: In establishing the initiative under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consider using the online Data Center Maturity Model.

children.heading: International specifications and metrics
children.label: h International specifications and metrics
children.text: The Secretary, in collaboration with key stakeholders, shall actively participate in efforts to harmonize global specifications and metrics for data center energy and water efficiency.

children.heading: Data center utilization metric
children.label: i Data center utilization metric
children.text: The Secretary, in collaboration with key stakeholders, shall facilitate in the development of an efficiency metric that measures the energy efficiency of a data center (including equipment and facilities).

children.heading: Protection of proprietary information
children.label: j Protection of proprietary information
children.text: The Secretary and the Administrator shall not disclose any proprietary information or trade secrets provided by any individual or company for the purposes of carrying out this section or the programs and initiatives established under this section.
